question: Among Texas cities with diabetes prevalence above 12% in each year from the year from 2011-2016 that has the highest total passenger count across Los Angeles International Airport, which two had 2020 populations under 100,000, and which city borders both of them? Only report the city name. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Summing Passenger_Count by year (from ReportPeriod) across all terminals and categories for 2011-2016, the highest total is 2016 with 80921651 passengers.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the highest total passenger in Los Angeles international airports? (Convert ReportPeriod to Year; sum Passenger_Count by Year; select the maximum.)

2.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2016 release, 10 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
2.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the year from <node_1 answer>?

3.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2017 release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Baytown, Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
3.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in 2017?

4.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2018 release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, and Tyler.
4.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in 2018?

5.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities 2019 release, 13 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, Tyler, and Waco.
5.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in 2019?

6.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for All Texas Places, the following 2020 Census populations (POP100 field) are recorded for the 9 cities from the intersection: Beaumont (115,282), Brownsville (186,738), Corpus Christi (317,863), El Paso (678,815), Laredo (255,205), McAllen (142,210), Mission (85,778), Pharr (79,715), and San Antonio (1,434,625). Only Mission and Pharr have populations under 100,000.
6.subquestion: Which cities from <intersection of nodes 2-5> had population under 100,000 in the 2020 Census?

7.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Pharr, Texas, the city 'borders McAllen to the west, Edinburg to the north, San Juan to the east, and Hidalgo to the southwest.'
7.subquestion: Which cities border <city from node 6: Pharr>?

8.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Mission, Texas, the city borders 'McAllen to the east, Palmhurst to the north, and Palmview to the west.'
8.subquestion: Which cities border <city from node 6: Mission>?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with highest LAX passenger count (2011-2016) -> 2016
HOP 2 (d=4, CDC 500 Cities diabetes data 2016-2019 - nodes 2-5):
Node 2: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2016 release -> 10 cities
Node 3: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2017 release -> 12 cities
Node 4: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2018 release -> 12 cities
Node 5: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2019 release -> 13 cities
Intersection of nodes 2-5 -> 9 cities (Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio)
Node 6: Filter 9-city intersection by 2020 Census pop < 100k -> Mission (85,778) and Pharr (79,715)
Node 7 (Wikipedia Pharr,_Texas): Cities bordering Pharr -> McAllen, Edinburg, San Juan, Hidalgo
Node 8 (Wikipedia Mission,_Texas): Cities bordering Mission -> McAllen, Palmhurst, Palmview
Intersection: City that borders BOTH Pharr and Mission -> McAllen
